diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sharp, pleuritic quality is typical of pericarditis rather than the pressure/heaviness of ischaemia.
Sharp quality is less typical of ACS but does not exclude atypical presentations.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain worse lying flat and on deep inspiration, relieved by sitting forward

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positional pain (worse supine, relieved leaning forward) is one of the most characteristic features of pericarditis.
Ischaemic pain is not typically positional, but this does not exclude ACS on its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Radiation of pain to the trapezius ridges (tops of shoulders)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Trapezius-ridge radiation reflects phrenic-nerve innervation of the pericardium and is relatively specific for pericarditis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent viral prodrome (sore throat, myalgias) ~1 week prior

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A preceding viral illness is the commonest antecedent of viral/idiopathic pericarditis.
The same viral trigger can produce myocardial involvement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low-grade fever / feels feverish (37.8 degrees, home thermometer)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Low-grade fever is common in inflammatory pericarditis but non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pericardial friction rub (triphasic, left lower sternal border, loudest leaning forward in expiration)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A triphasic pericardial rub is highly specific and is one of the four diagnostic criteria — but it requires auscultation and is unobtainable via telehealth (sealed clinician finding).

symptoms.symptom_name: Widespread ST elevation with PR depression on 12-lead ECG

investigation_details.expected_result: Diffuse, non-territorial concave ST elevation with PR-segment depression
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot confirm pericarditis nor exclude an ST-elevation ACS pattern — the single most important discriminating test.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse ST elevation with PR depression is characteristic and distinguishes pericarditis from territorial STEMI.
An ECG is mandatory to separate diffuse pericarditic changes from a territorial infarct pattern.

investigation_details.expected_result: Normal in isolated pericarditis; elevated if concurrent myocarditis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot exclude ACS or detect myopericardial involvement that would contraindicate NSAIDs.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal serial troponin is central to excluding ACS.
A raised troponin signals myocardial involvement and changes management.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR ~105, out of proportion to low-grade fever)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sinus tachycardia accompanies pain/fever, but being out of proportion should prompt consideration of PE or myocarditis. The precise measured rate is a sealed clinician value; the AI can only work from the patient-reported racing pulse.
Unexplained tachycardia is a soft PE signal.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain NOT crushing/pressure, no arm/jaw radiation, no diaphoresis or nausea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of the classic ischaemic quality lowers ACS probability but cannot exclude it without objective testing.

symptoms.symptom_name: No calf pain/swelling, no haemoptysis, no sudden rest dyspnoea (PE history screen)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduces PE probability, but pleuritic pain plus tachycardia keeps PE on the differential pending objective exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear lung fields, no S3/S4 gallop, no raised JVP (examination negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No raised JVP argues against a large effusion/tamponade at this moment — but this is an in-person examination finding, sealed and unavailable to the telehealth AI.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Positional pleuritic pain + trapezius radiation + viral prodrome + friction rub is close to pathognomonic — yet two of these (the rub, and the ECG) are only obtainable in person, so the cluster cannot be completed via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most important discriminator between pericarditis and a territorial ACS; must be same-day and in person.
Excludes ACS and detects concurrent myocarditis, which would contraindicate high-dose NSAIDs.
Supports the inflammatory diagnosis and provides a baseline to guide anti-inflammatory duration and taper.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: PE assessment (Wells score +/- D-dimer or CTPA)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Pleuritic pain with tachycardia warrants structured PE exclusion once in person.
Assesses for pericardial effusion/tamponade and ventricular function if myocarditis is suspected.
Screens for an enlarged cardiac silhouette (effusion), pneumonia or other thoracic causes.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical high-dose NSAID prescription without prior ECG/troponin
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Starting anti-inflammatory therapy remotely before excluding ACS and myocarditis risks masking or worsening a dangerous mimic.
